response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with deep TypeScript/Node.js and notable DLang systems experience, but the role requires 8-12 years of hands-on C++ development for industrial automation. His C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a university thesis project, with no evidence of STL-heavy modern C++, message brokers, MQTT, industrial protocols, or IEC 62443 / SDL secure-development experience. The low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby shows relevant systems-programming aptitude, but the core skill mismatch is too large for this specialized position.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers) transferable to performance-critical work
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months, over a decade ago) versus the required 8-12 years
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, or industrial protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) background
No demonstrated Secure Development Lifecycle or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity experience

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / security standards exposure
